title: The line that closes more leases than any pitch
dek: Most agents talk their way out of a signed lease. Here is the four-second alternative.
heroAlt: The line that closes more leases than any pitch

blocks.html: A lease dies in the silence after the tour. The client loved the unit, loved the building, loved the view. Then they said the five words every agent hears: we want to think about it.
Most agents respond by talking. More amenities, more urgency, more pitch. And every extra sentence tells the client the same thing: this person needs the deal more than I need the apartment.

blocks.text: The agent who is comfortable in the silence is the agent who gets the signature.

blocks.html: Here is what to say instead, and it takes four seconds: name the hesitation, then stop. That is the whole move. You are not pressuring. You are proving you can be trusted with the truth.
On your next tour, when you hear we want to think about it, say: totally fair. What is the one thing you would change about this unit? Then say nothing until they answer.

items.title: It surfaces the real objection.
items.html: Think about it is never the actual reason. The question forces the truth into the open where you can handle it.

items.title: Silence transfers the pressure.
items.html: Whoever speaks first after the question owns the objection. Let it be them.

items.title: It positions you as the advisor.
items.html: Advisors ask questions. Salespeople pitch. Clients sign with advisors.

blocks.lead: Want the full objection playbook, word for word?
